body_txt: You're miserable. You're unpopular. Your erstwhile Best Friend Forever just got a look from the Alpha Bitch and charged off to join the Girl Posse without a backwards glance. The Jerk Jock is harassing you to prove that Youth Is Wasted on the Dumb . You have too much homework and you've put it off too late. You have no time and no qualifications to make money, so you can afford nothing. So your parents burble, "These are the best years of your life!" A Stock Phrase frequently deployed by nostalgic adults who want to think that Growing Up Sucks but childhood was good. Generally indicates that Adults Are Useless , as few if any stories exist where the adults are actually right to say this. When an adult is reflecting on the best years of their life, the trope is Glory Days . Overlap is likely. Truth in Television —not the phrase itself, mind you. Don't take this phrase to heart. It is Glurge that makes it sound like there's nothing worth living for after graduating, which is simply false, hence why this trope never reflects well on the one saying it. But it is true that adults like to say this to high school kids, despite the fact that telling this to a high schooler is very liable to only get across the message that their Sucky School which they're probably ready to be done with is supposedly the best life has to offer—and no kid's going to feel ready for adulthood if they think it will be worse . The only character for whom this is actually true is the Future Loser . For everyone else, there are reasons why Growing Up Sucks and reasons why it doesn't (see that trope's page), so anyone saying school days are the best days of one's life clearly never went to school. Or saw the show , for that matter. The Anime /Japanese culture equivalent is seishun (springtime of your youth). Generally played even more melodramatically (and parodied all the time).
